title: Fixing React Memoized Component Re-renders on Object Reference Change

statement.markdown:
In this task, you will be fixing a React memoized component that incorrectly re-renders when the object reference changes. The component should only re-render when the object properties change.

You will be given a state with an object and a function to update the state. The function should create a new object reference to the state object when updating its properties.

Your task is to fix the memoized component to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a JSON object with two properties: `state` and `updateState`. The `state` property will be an object with any number of properties, and the `updateState` property will be a function that takes the state object as an argument.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be an object with two properties: `newState` and `isMemoized`. The `newState` property should be the updated state object, and the `isMemoized` property should be a boolean indicating whether the component is memoized.
statement.notes: The `updateState` function should be called with the current state object, and it should return the updated state object. If the `updateState` function returns the same object reference as the current state object, the component should be memoized.

constraints.description: Number of properties in the state object

examples.explanation: The component should not be memoized because the updateState function returns the same object reference as the current state object.
The component should not be memoized because the updateState function changes the object reference.

hints.text: Check if the updateState function is creating a new object reference to the state object.

editorial.title: Fixing React Memoized Component Re-renders on Object Reference Change
editorial.intuition: The component should only re-render when the object properties change. To achieve this, we need to create a new object reference to the state object when updating its properties.
editorial.bruteForce: We can create a new object reference to the state object using the spread operator or the Object.assign method.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use the immer library to create a new object reference to the state object.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use the immer library to create a new object reference to the state object and then update the properties of the new object.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component will only re-render when the object properties change.
